task_description: Search for a one-way first-class flight for 1 traveler from Shenzhen to Shanghai on the selected departure date.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the Expedia category tabs, and the semitransparent red X is centered on the Flights tab, identified by a small airplane icon above the label “Flights.” Nearby tabs include Stays on the left and Cars on the right. In the full-screen view, this tab row sits at the top of the main Expedia booking panel above destination, dates, travelers, and Search fields, indicating this is a top-area UI control rather than content on the page.
caption.action: Click the Flights tab with the airplane icon.
caption.expectation: The Flights tab should become selected, switching the booking form into flight-search mode.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the right side of a rounded rectangular input field labeled “Travelers, Cabin class,” with the current value “1 traveler, Economy.” A semitransparent red X is centered on the value text inside this field, indicating the target is that travelers and cabin class selector. In the full-screen view, this is a UI control in the top booking form on Expedia’s Flights search panel, positioned between the Date field and the blue Search button.
caption.action: Click the “Travelers, Cabin class” field showing “1 traveler, Economy.”
caption.expectation: The travelers and cabin class selector should open, allowing adjustment of passenger counts and cabin class.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the lower part of the open Travelers and Cabin class popover. A semitransparent red X is centered on the rectangular Cabin class dropdown field, which currently displays “Economy” and has a downward chevron on the right; the blue Done button is directly below it. In the full-screen view, this popover is open from the Expedia Flights search form near the top of the page, with passenger counters above and the rest of the booking form and page content dimly visible behind it. This is a UI control inside the open selector panel.
caption.action: Click the Cabin class dropdown field showing “Economy.”
caption.expectation: The cabin class menu should open, showing available fare class options to choose from.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows an open cabin class dropdown list. The semitransparent red X is centered on the bottom option, “First class,” which is shown on a dark gray highlighted row beneath “Business class.” In the full-screen view, this dropdown is inside the open “Travelers and Cabin class” popover on Expedia’s Flights search form, so the target is a UI control menu option rather than page content.
caption.action: Click the “First class” option in the Cabin class dropdown list.
caption.expectation: The “First class” option should become selected, the dropdown should close, and the cabin class field should update to show First class.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the lower-right area of the open Travelers and Cabin class popover. A semitransparent red X is centered on the blue rounded button labeled “Done,” indicating the exact target is that button. In the full-screen view, this popover is open on Expedia’s Flights search form, with the cabin class already set to “First class” and the page’s main flight fields visible behind it. This is a UI control inside the panel.
caption.action: Click the blue “Done” button in the Travelers and Cabin class popover.
caption.expectation: The popover should close, saving the current traveler and cabin class selection, and the flight search form should remain visible with the updated “1 traveler, First class” value.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the flight trip-type tabs, with a semitransparent red X centered on the middle tab labeled “One-way.” The neighboring tabs “Roundtrip” and “Multi-city” are visible, and the target is a UI control tab in the top area of the flight form. In the full-screen view, this is Expedia’s Flights search panel, with the flights category already open, a leaving-from field filled with “Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.),” an empty destination field, date and traveler/cabin class fields, and a blue Search button on the right.
caption.action: Click the “One-way” tab in the flight search form.
caption.expectation: The one-way trip type should become selected, updating the form to one-way mode and adjusting the date section accordingly.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the flight search form with the semitransparent red X centered inside the left input field labeled "Leaving from," on the existing text "Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)." The field has a blue outline, indicating it is an interactive text-entry control. In the full-screen view, this is Expedia’s Flights search panel with the "One-way" tab selected, a "Going to" destination field to the right, date and traveler fields, and a blue "Search" button. The target is a UI control in the top search area.
caption.action: Click inside the "Leaving from" field containing "Guangzhou (CAN-Baiyun Intl.)."
caption.expectation: The departure field should become active for editing, likely placing a text cursor in the field and preparing location suggestions or edit mode for the origin airport.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the active "Leaving from" text-entry field in Expedia's flight search form. The field has a blue outline, a location-pin icon on the left, a small clear-entry X icon on the right, and the typed text "shenzhe" visible inside, indicating the new departure city is being entered and replacing the previous airport value. A dropdown suggestion panel is open directly below the field, currently showing placeholder loading rows. In the full-screen view, the Flights section is selected with the "One-way" tab active, an empty "Going to" field beside it, the date field showing "Thu, Apr 30," a traveler field showing "1 traveler, Economy," and a blue "Search" button on the right.
caption.action: Type "SHENZHEN" into the active "Leaving from" field.
caption.expectation: The departure field should update to the full typed city name and the suggestion list below should refresh to show matching Shenzhen airport or city options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the semitransparent red X centered on the first autocomplete suggestion in the dropdown beneath the departure field. The highlighted row reads "Shenzhen (SZX - All Airports)" with "China" underneath, and it has an airplane-style icon on the left, indicating a selectable airport/city suggestion. In the full-screen view, this is Expedia's Flights search form with "One-way" selected, the "Leaving from" field containing "shenzhen," the suggestion list open below it, an empty "Going to" field, the date set to "Thu, Apr 30," traveler details to the right, and a blue "Search" button. The click target is a UI control in the suggestion list.
caption.action: Click the highlighted suggestion "Shenzhen (SZX - All Airports)" in the dropdown under the "Leaving from" field.
caption.expectation: The suggestion should be selected, the dropdown should close, and the departure field should update to the Shenzhen all-airports option.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the active "Going to" destination text field outlined in blue with a location-pin icon on the left and a small clear-entry X icon on the right. The text entry area contains the partially typed destination "shangha," indicating the user is entering Shanghai and building on the existing typed text rather than replacing it. Directly below, an autocomplete dropdown is open with destination suggestions including "Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)," "Shanghai (PVG - Pudong Intl.)," and "Shanghai (SHA - Hongqiao Intl.)." In the full-screen view, this is Expedia's Flights search form with "One-way" selected, the departure field already set to "Shenzhen, China (SZX-All Air...)," the date set to "Thu, Apr 30," traveler details shown, and a blue "Search" button on the right.
caption.action: Type "SHANGHAI" into the active "Going to" field.
caption.expectation: The destination field should update to the full typed city name and the suggestion list should refresh or remain open with matching Shanghai airport and city options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the first autocomplete suggestion in the destination dropdown. The highlighted row reads "Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)" with "China" beneath it and an airplane-style location icon on the left, indicating a selectable suggestion item. In the full-screen view, this is Expedia's Flights search form with "One-way" selected, the departure field already set to Shenzhen, the "Going to" field containing "shanghai," the suggestion list open below it, the date set to "Thu, Apr 30," traveler details shown, and a blue "Search" button on the right.
caption.action: Click the highlighted suggestion "Shanghai (SHA - All Airports)" in the dropdown under the "Going to" field.
caption.expectation: The destination suggestion should be selected, the dropdown should close, and the "Going to" field should update to the Shanghai all-airports option.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the date cell labeled "15," which is a circular outlined day button in the calendar. In the full-screen view, Expedia's Flights search page is open with a date-picker panel showing April 2026 and May 2026 side by side; April 30 is currently filled blue as the selected date, and the clicked "15" is another selectable day within the calendar grid. This is a UI control inside the date-picker dialog.
caption.action: Click the day "15" in the calendar date picker.
caption.expectation: The clicked date should become selected in the calendar, updating the highlighted travel date and keeping the date-picker open or ready for confirmation.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dark blue rounded button labeled "Done" at the bottom-right of the date-picker panel. In the full-screen view, Expedia's Flights search page is open in Microsoft Edge with the Flights tab active, One-way selected, Shenzhen as the departure, Shanghai as the destination, and a calendar popup showing Wed, Apr 15 selected. The target is a UI control in the date-picker dialog.
caption.action: Click the blue "Done" button in the date-picker panel.
caption.expectation: The selected date should be confirmed and the calendar popup should close, returning focus to the flight search form with the chosen travel date shown.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on a dark blue rounded rectangular button labeled "Search" in white text. It sits at the right end of the flight search form, indicating a clickable UI control. In the full-screen view, Microsoft Edge is open to Expedia's Flights page with the Flights tab active, One-way selected, Shenzhen as the departure, Shanghai as the destination, the date set to Wed, Apr 15, and traveler details set to 1 traveler, Economy.
caption.expectation: The flight search form should submit and Expedia should load the flight results page for the selected one-way trip.
